title: ee.status.search.lexical_ram_tier.v1
description: Lexical posting-list RAM-tier pinning posture surfaced under `ee status --json` at `data.search.lexicalRamTier`. Scaffold contract for bd-1hvzh / bd-21xbi; the wiring slice consumes this schema verbatim.

platform.description: Coarse host classification for the lexical RAM-tier optimization.

supported.description: True iff the platform supports the full `mmap + mlock + MADV_HUGEPAGE` triple. Linux only.

enabled.description: True iff the operator left `[search.lexical_ram_tier] enabled = true`.

attempted.description: True iff the loader actually invoked the pinning path. Must be false when `enabled=false`.

succeeded.description: True iff the lexical index files are now RAM-pinned with the requested posture. The scaffold never sets this to true; only the syscall slice does.

hugepagesRequested.description: True iff the operator asked for `MADV_HUGEPAGE`.

hugepagesGranted.description: True iff the kernel accepted the `MADV_HUGEPAGE` advice for at least one mapping. Always false in the scaffold.

populateRequested.description: True iff the operator asked for MAP_POPULATE / madvise(WILLNEED) at load time.

bytesMmapped.description: Total bytes mapped into RAM across all lexical index files. Always 0 in the scaffold.

pageFaultsPre.description: Process page-fault counter before the pinning attempt. 0 until the wiring slice reads `/proc/self/stat`.

pageFaultsPost.description: Process page-fault counter after the pinning attempt; the delta vs `pageFaultsPre` is the bd-21xbi acceptance signal that pinning eliminated first-touch faults.

fallbackPath.description: Coarse strategy the loader took when the optimization could not be fully applied. `none` is reserved for a successful full pin.

indexPath.description: Filesystem path of the lexical index directory the loader operated on, when known.

degradedCodes.description: Stable degraded[] codes documented in tests/fixtures/failure_modes/. Empty only when the optimization succeeded fully.
